narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD broad weakness because geopolitical de-escalation via US-Iran progress collapses safe-haven demand and DXY is confirming at 98.05 down 0.44% with US 10Y yields falling 1.40%
2. Risk-on rotation because equities up 1.22%, copper up 3.89%, and VIX down 2.13% are all pulling capital into commodity and growth-linked currencies simultaneously
3. Gold divergence creating a tension signal because gold surging 3.01% alongside silver up 6.10% contradicts pure risk-on and suggests residual USD distrust rather than clean sentiment reversal

PRICE CONFIRMS

NZDUSD leading the USD weakness theme with an H4 Strong Buy signal at CSM strength of 100, fully consistent with the DXY decline and risk-on macro backdrop. CADJPY is confirming the JPY recovery narrative from the cross side with an H4 Strong Sell signal, and USDJPY correlating at 0.96 with CADJPY means JPY crosses are moving as a coherent block. AUD is showing H4 CSM fade from D1 strength which suggests it is lagging NZD as the cleaner long vehicle, making NZDUSD the leading pair in this USD sell structure.

REGIME

Mixed leaning Risk-On, conflicted by Gold. VIX at 17.01 down 2.13% and S&P 500 up 1.22% confirm improving sentiment, but Gold up 3.01% and Silver up 6.10% indicate persistent USD distrust and residual safe-haven hedging that prevents a clean risk-on designation.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus equities: Gold surging 3.01% alongside S&P 500 up 1.22% is internally inconsistent because true risk-on should suppress gold not elevate it simultaneously
2. AUD CSM divergence: AUD ranks highest on D1 CSM at 90 but is fading sharply on H4 at 47, conflicting with its high correlation to NZD which is strengthening on both timeframes

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: NZDUSD up - strongest CSM, H4 Strong Buy, USD weak, risk-on aligned - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: CADJPY down - H4 Strong Sell, JPY recovering, CAD weakest currency cross-asset - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: AUDUSD - H4 CSM divergence from D1 creates timing ambiguity and conflicted entry
Watch: DXY breaking and holding below 97.80 confirms primary NZDUSD trade and invalidates any USD recovery thesis

themes.theme: US-Iran diplomatic progress and nuclear deal hopes
Oil price collapse on geopolitical de-escalation
Risk-on sentiment supporting equity markets and commodity currencies
Fed messaging on inflation and productivity remaining uncertain
Central bank divergence with BoK caution and SEK strength
Safe-haven flows reversing as risk appetite improves
LatAm hedging demand shift with Banxico policy focus

key_observation: Dominant theme is US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough crushing safe-haven demand and oil prices. Dollar weakness broad-based across majors (GBP, EUR, AUD all rallying) as equity markets surge to record highs on peace hopes. Risk-on environment is fully engaged with commodity currencies performing well. Fed messaging on inflation remains conflicted with no clear directional conviction.
watch: Non-farm payroll data due Friday will be critical test of USD support given current weakness. Monitor Iran-US negotiations for any headlines that could reverse positioning. Oil price floor around 65-70 USD/bbl given demand destruction concerns. Watch JPY intervention risks if yen weakness accelerates. Banxico policy decision implications for LatAm FX hedging flows.
